title: Mildly Yeasty Ukrainian Dough Dumplings
description: A yeasty, fluffy, and quick-fix Ukrainian dish featuring paprika, perfect for a weeknight dinner, and ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly for a balanced meal.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, yeast, paprika, salt, and sugar.
Gradually add the warm water to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Let the dough rise in a warm place for 30 minutes, or until it has doubled in size.
Preheat a large pot of boiling water and add the namul.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and shape each piece into a ball.
Carefully add the dough balls to the boiling water and cook for 10 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
Remove the dumplings from the water with a slotted spoon and serve with olive oil and additional namul if desired.
